assertions.description: SharedWorker constructor is exposed — also affected by the spec-aligned error-event behaviour.
Worker.prototype exposes addEventListener so the async 'error' event delivered for CSP-blocked workers can be observed.

Per the spec change, constructing a Worker that will ultimately be CSP-blocked must not throw synchronously — the failure is delivered later via an 'error' event. We probe by trying a regular data: URL worker construction; it should not throw at construction time.
Document still fires SecurityPolicyViolationEvent for CSP violations — the error event on the worker complements (not replaces) the violation report.
ErrorEvent constructor is exposed; this is the interface dispatched on the worker when CSP blocks its fetch.
